name: Extrude POP
description: The Extrude POP is a gives apparent "thickness" to line strips, triangles or quads. It copies the line strip, triangle or quad at a specifiable separation (Distance), optionally shrinks it to give a taper (Inset), and adds quads for the sides. You must pass it to a [[Normal POP]] to render it with faceted shading. Each point of the line strips can have a unique corresponding Distance of the extrusion via the Map page. See also [[Triangulate POP]].

parameters.name: Group
parameters.label: Group
parameters.description: If there are input groups, specifying a group name in this field will cause this POP to act only upon the group specified.

parameters.name: Taper
parameters.label: Taper
parameters.description: Applies a scaling factor to extruded geometry.

parameters.name: Per Edge
parameters.label: Per Edge
parameters.description: When on, faces are extruded as a group instead of individually. Useful in particular when extruding an input group.

parameters.name: Max Primitives per Point
parameters.label: Max Primitives per Point
parameters.description: Used to allocate temporary storage when Keep Faces Connected is on, it should be set to the maximum number a primitives a point can be a part of in the input geometry.

parameters.name: Copy Topology Info Back to CPU
parameters.label: Copy Topology Info Back to CPU
parameters.description: Enable copying the point count and topology information held on the GPU to the CPU.

_provenance.fact: parameters and description scraped from the live Extrude_POP wiki page ({{Parameter}}/{{ParameterItem}} templates)
